description: Server-side session validation for the separation module: session table, login generates sessionId, getSessionUser helper, client sends X-Session-Id header, rate limiting per session

userStories.title: Migration: create siso_sessoes table
userStories.description: As the system, I need a sessions table to persist server-side session data so that user identity can be validated on every API request.
acceptanceCriteria: Create migration file supabase/migrations/20260311_create_siso_sessoes.sql

CREATE TABLE siso_sessoes with columns: id (uuid PK default gen_random_uuid()), usuario_id (uuid NOT NULL FK siso_usuarios), criado_em (timestamptz default now()), expira_em (timestamptz default now() + interval '12 hours')
Create partial index idx_sessoes_expira ON siso_sessoes (expira_em) WHERE expira_em > now()
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create getSessionUser helper in src/lib/session.ts
userStories.description: As the system, I need a helper that validates a session ID from the request header and returns the authenticated user with their resolved galpão.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new file src/lib/session.ts
Export interface SessionUser with fields: id (string), nome (string), cargo (string), galpaoId (string | null)
Export async function getSessionUser(request: Request): Promise<SessionUser | null>
Function reads X-Session-Id from request headers
Function queries siso_sessoes JOIN siso_usuarios WHERE id = sessionId AND expira_em > now()
Function resolves galpaoId from cargo: operador_cwb → query siso_galpoes WHERE nome = 'CWB', operador_sp → query siso_galpoes WHERE nome = 'SP', admin/comprador → null
Returns null if session not found, expired, or user not found
Uses createServiceClient() from supabase-server.ts for DB access
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Login endpoint generates and returns sessionId
userStories.description: As the system, I need the login endpoint to create a server-side session so that subsequent requests can be validated.
acceptanceCriteria: In POST /api/auth/login (src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts), after validating PIN and finding user, insert a new row into siso_sessoes with usuario_id
Add sessionId (the generated UUID) to the existing JSON response alongside id, nome, cargo
Existing login response fields (id, nome, cargo) remain unchanged — fully backwards compatible
If siso_sessoes insert fails, log warning but still return successful login (session is enhancement, not blocker)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Client stores sessionId and sends X-Session-Id header
userStories.description: As the system, I need the client to persist the sessionId and include it in all API requests so that the server can validate the user's identity.
acceptanceCriteria: In auth-context.tsx, after successful login, store sessionId in localStorage as part of the siso_user object
Expose sessionId in the auth context (useAuth hook returns sessionId)
Create and export a helper function sisoFetch(url, options?) that wraps fetch and automatically adds X-Session-Id header from stored sessionId
sisoFetch falls back to regular fetch if no sessionId is stored (backwards compatible)
Existing auth flow (localStorage-based, no JWT) continues to work unchanged
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Session-based rate limiter for bip endpoint
userStories.description: As the system, I need per-session rate limiting to prevent scanner malfunction from flooding the bip API.
acceptanceCriteria: Create rate limiting logic in src/lib/session.ts (or export from a new utility if session.ts gets too large)
Export function checkBipRateLimit(sessionId: string): { allowed: boolean; retryAfterMs?: number }
Limit: max 2 requests per second per sessionId
Implementation: in-memory Map<sessionId, { count: number, resetAt: number }>
Cleanup: remove entries older than 60 seconds every 100 calls to prevent memory leak
Returns allowed: false with retryAfterMs when limit exceeded
Typecheck passes
